executive_summary: Heavy Markets week with 8 high-urgency quant trading repos surfacing—your NIFTY options backtesting infrastructure could leapfrog this week. Hyperswitch stands out in Payments as a serious multi-rail orchestration alternative for Redpin. No direct AI/Efficiency or Creator picks this cycle, but VeighNa and OpenBB show cross-domain patterns applicable to payment analytics. Signal is strong but concentrated; prioritize the 2-3 tools that solve your immediate backtesting bottleneck.

top_picks.headline: Production-grade NIFTY options backtester, battle-tested
top_picks.why_now: Your first-5-minute candle CE/PE strategy needs historical validation before scaling capital; Lean's options module handles exactly this with institutional-grade accuracy.
top_picks.action: Pull Lean's options module this week, load 6 months of NIFTY options data, run your candle strategy against it—target 100 backtest runs by Friday.

top_picks.headline: Cut Redpin's single-rail risk with orchestration layer
top_picks.why_now: If your primary processor goes down, you're dead; Hyperswitch lets you failover across Razorpay/PayU/Stripe in milliseconds without rewriting integration code.
top_picks.action: Spin up Hyperswitch sandbox, connect 2 test processors, simulate a failover scenario—document latency delta vs current setup.

top_picks.headline: Microsecond tick storage for options replay
top_picks.why_now: Your backtests are only as good as your data granularity; QuestDB handles millions of ticks without choking, enabling true first-candle reconstruction.
top_picks.action: Deploy QuestDB locally, ingest 1 week of NIFTY options tick data from your broker feed, verify query latency under 10ms for candle aggregations.

top_picks.headline: Free Greeks and options chains, ditch paid APIs
top_picks.why_now: You're likely paying for options data that OpenBB aggregates for free; the derivatives module calculates Greeks on-the-fly for position sizing.
top_picks.action: Install OpenBB, pull NIFTY options chain for current expiry, compare Greeks output against your broker's data—validate accuracy before switching.

top_picks.headline: Rust-speed backtesting if Lean feels slow
top_picks.why_now: If your backtest runs are taking hours, NautilusTrader's Rust core can cut that to minutes—worth evaluating as Lean alternative.
top_picks.action: Bookmark for next week; only pursue if Lean backtest performance becomes a bottleneck after initial runs.

skip_list.reason: China-focused, CTP gateway dependencies make NIFTY integration unnecessarily complex vs Lean/Nautilus
Curated list, not actionable tool—you already have the specific repos you need from this batch
Multi-agent complexity overkill for your single-strategy backtest needs; revisit when scaling to multiple concurrent strategies
